slides.title: PEDRRA Training Exercise
slides.subtitle: PErsonal Data bReach Risk Assessment
slides.content: Welcome to the training. Please follow along closely as we begin our interactive scenarios on Data Breach Risk Management.

slides.title: Welcome & Context
slides.subtitle: Learning Objectives
slides.content: Not every security incident is a data breach, but every data breach is a security incident. Today we enhance practical skills in Risk Assessment and inter-disciplinary communication between IT, DPOs, and Management.

slides.title: Scenario 1: The Encrypted Gateway
slides.subtitle: Stolen Laptop
slides.content: An external consultant's laptop holding 150 expert contacts was stolen from a locked home office overnight. It has full-disk encryption and a strong passphrase. A remote wipe was initiated, but remains pending connection.

slides.title: Scenario 1 Assessment
slides.question: What is the initial risk assessment for the 150 experts?

slides.title: Lecture: Data Breach Essentials
slides.subtitle: EDPS Perspective
slides.content: A personal data breach is a security breach leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure or access to personal data. Note the strict 72-hour EDPS notification deadline!

slides.title: Knowledge Check
slides.question: Your agency must notify the EDPS...
options: Only if the breach is High Risk.

slides.title: Scenario 2: Inter-Agency Data Leak
slides.subtitle: Cloud Service Compromise
slides.content: CloudVault was breached via phishing. 750,000 pseudonymized records were accessed containing demographic data and a 'health status indicator'. Data was NOT encrypted at rest, a severe violation of the processor's DPA.

slides.title: Scenario 2 Assesssment
slides.question: Which specific factor most strongly elevates this leak to 'High Risk' status?

slides.title: Final Debrief & Feedback
slides.subtitle: End of PEDRRA Exercise
slides.content: Thank you for participating. We will now reflect on inter-disciplinary collaboration, strategic decision-making, and fill out our evaluation forms.

questions.text: How would you rate the training? (1-5)
